title: Sweet and Sour Cupcake Power!
story: Bakers everywhere whip up the perfect combo of sweet and tangy on December 15 for National Lemon Cupcake Day! These sunny yellow treats pack a citrus punch that makes your taste buds dance. People celebrate by baking fluffy lemon cupcakes topped with creamy frosting, sharing photos of their creations online, and treating friends to these zesty delights. Bakeries go all out with special lemon cupcake flavors like lemon blueberry or lemon cream cheese. Some families make it a tradition to bake together, filling their kitchens with the fresh scent of lemons and vanilla.
activity_question: What makes lemon cupcakes special compared to regular cupcakes?
activity_choices: They have a sweet and tangy citrus flavor
They can only be eaten with a spoon
activity_answer: They have a sweet and tangy citrus flavor
bonus_fact: Lemons contain natural oils in their peels that make cupcakes extra fragrant and flavorful!

title: Speaking the Same Language Around the World
story: Every December 15, language lovers celebrate Zamenhof Day by speaking, learning, and sharing Esperanto - a special language created to help people from different countries talk to each other! Dr. Zamenhof invented this amazing language over 100 years ago, mixing words from many languages to make something everyone could learn easily. Today, thousands of people post Esperanto messages online, attend virtual meetups, and teach friends basic phrases like "saluton" (hello) and "dankon" (thank you). It's like having a secret code that connects you to people everywhere!
bonus_fact: Esperanto uses only 16 grammar rules, making it much easier than most languages!

title: Cat Herders Day: The Impossible Job Holiday
story: Every December 15, people celebrate Cat Herders Day by honoring anyone who tries to do the impossible! This silly holiday is all about recognizing folks who tackle really tough jobs - like teachers managing wild classrooms, parents getting kids ready for school, or anyone trying to organize a group project. People share funny memes about "herding cats" on social media and give high-fives to friends with challenging jobs. Some offices even have "Cat Herder of the Year" awards for the person who keeps everyone organized. It's a day to laugh about life's chaos and appreciate the people who somehow make impossible things happen anyway!
activity_question: What do people do on Cat Herders Day to show appreciation?
activity_choices: Give high-fives to friends with challenging jobs
Actually try to herd real cats in parks
activity_answer: Give high-fives to friends with challenging jobs
bonus_fact: The phrase "herding cats" means trying to control something that's basically uncontrollable!
